forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over NSW will continue to move eastwards to be centred over the Tasman Sea by Monday. Northerly winds freshen over the state on Tuesday ahead of a weakening cold front mid-week, with a second cold front expected late in the week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Isolated fog patches about eastern parts of the northern agricultural area. Mostly dry. Maximum temperatures generally above average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, freshening in the west.
Mostly clear. Northeast to northerly winds.

Showers developing about the west during the day. Isolated showers about the northeast. Maximum temperatures above average. Freshening north to northwesterly winds, shifting west to southwesterly in the far southwest in the evening.

Showers about central and eastern parts of the agricultural area, contracting eastwards and clearing later. Maximum temperatures above average, tending below average in the southwest. North to northwesterly winds, ahead of a cooler west to southwesterly change about western and central parts, extending throughout apart from the far northeast during the day.

text.p: Mainly dry, apart from possible isolated showers in the far southwest later. Maximum temperatures above average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, freshening in the west.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Thursday: Generally less than 5 mm about the agricultural area and west, increasing to 5 to 10 mm about the southwest and western coasts of Eyre Peninsula and Kangaroo Island, with isolated falls of 10 to 20 mm possible in these areas.
